title: op environment read hangs from Python subprocess on Windows
description: The beta 1Password CLI (v2.35.0-beta.01) hangs indefinitely when called from Python subprocess.run with any stdout/stderr handle redirection. Root cause: op requires console attachment (STARTF_USESTDHANDLES strips it). Affects get_secret_from_environment() in auth.py when OP_ENVIRONMENT_ID is set — op environment read and op run both timeout, so the ClickUp API key is never loaded and the user is prompted instead. Fix: add _reexec_under_op_run() in main.py to re-exec the script under op run --environment without handle redirection, letting op inject secrets into the subprocess env. By the time main() runs, CLICKUP_API_KEY is already in os.environ at line 270.
close_reason: Fixed in PR #133 — _reexec_under_op_run() added to main.py

title: Release v1.05: finalize changelog and build EXE
description: The [Unreleased] block in docs/CHANGELOG.md already has entries (kfj_task_extractor.py standalone script, gspread dep, 1Password Environment auth). Steps: (1) move [Unreleased] to [1.05] with today's date, (2) bump version.py to 1.05, (3) update README version badge, (4) create release/v1.05 branch, (5) build EXE via ClickUpTaskExtractor.spec into dist/v1.05/, (6) tag v1.05 and push.
close_reason: Release PR #128 open; merge after #126 and #127

title: Guard type_config access in LocationMapper.map_location()
description: In mappers.py, LocationMapper.map_location() accesses option dicts without checking that type_config and its options list exist and are well-formed. A malformed or missing type_config from the ClickUp API causes a KeyError at runtime. Add a guard (check for None/missing keys) and fall back gracefully to the raw value.

title: Add GitHub Actions pytest CI workflow
description: Add .github/workflows/tests.yml to run pytest on push/PR to main. Triggered by GitHub issue #122. Simple first pass: ubuntu-latest, Python 3.11, install requirements.txt, run pytest tests/ -v.

title: Make ClickUpAPIClient request timeout configurable
description: api_client.py line ~108 has the request timeout hardcoded to 30s. Slow ClickUp API responses (large workspaces, high latency) can cause spurious failures. Expose a configurable timeout via ClickUpConfig or a constructor param, keeping 30s as the default.
